prompt_preview: what do you think about the current architecture?
note: opinion-seeking; advisory-means-no-edits memory still applies

note: v1.17.0 non-coding mixed: writing (draft+memo bigram + memo unigram) ≥ 2 AND operations (prepare+agenda bigram + agenda unigram) ≥ 2 → mixed via the new floor=2 rule

prompt_preview: compose a recap of the Q3 review for the leadership update

note: Pattern 6 negative — make sure | template ask, not council

prompt_preview: /ulw what should we do next to further improve the project
note: Pattern 3 loosened — multi-word bridge between we and improve triggers COUNCIL detection (independent axis); bare question form → intent advisory

prompt_preview: /ulw can the status line be further enhanced for better ux? For instance, adding the information when will the limits be reset. Implement and then commit as needed.
note: v1.23.0 fix — implementation-verb-led conjunction with destructive verb tail. Was 'advisory' under v1.22.x because the leading 'can the status line be ...' question dominated and the existing tail-imperative branch required a '. , ? \n' boundary. New is_imperative_request branch keyed on impl-verb head + (and|,) conjunction + destructive verb + object-marker tail (now including 'as needed', 'when ready', 'when stable') catches this natural-English shape. Forensics: session cf10ddd2-… commit cff1ee5.

prompt_preview: Implement and commit when ready
note: v1.23.0 — minimal impl-verb + and + destructive + temporal-marker

prompt_preview: Build it, then push to origin
note: v1.23.0 — comma conjunction with transition + destination object-marker (build/push without coding nouns scores general)

prompt_preview: Refactor X and tag v2.0
note: v1.23.0 — impl-verb + and + tag + version-shaped object-marker (refactor scores coding)

prompt_preview: Review the auth code and ship the fix
note: v1.27.0 F-001 — investigation-verb-led conjunction (review/audit/plan/evaluate) now matches when paired with destructive tail. Was 'advisory' under v1.26.x because review was excluded from bare-imperative and not in impl-verb-led conjunction list.

prompt_preview: Audit the codebase and commit fixes
note: v1.27.0 F-001 — audit + and + commit + object-marker.

prompt_preview: Do option C
note: v1.28.1 delegated-approval pattern — replies to multi-option proposals must classify as execution because the destructive verbs live in the prior assistant message, not the user's text

prompt_preview: Do option 1 as you recommended
note: v1.28.1 delegated-approval pattern — same shape as 'Do option C', longer phrasing

prompt_preview: Execute the plan
note: v1.28.1 delegated-approval — execute + the + plan referent; classifier scores domain=operations because 'execute'+'plan' both register as ops keywords

prompt_preview: Go with approach 2
note: v1.28.1 delegated-approval — go with + approach + identifier

prompt_preview: Proceed with the recommendation
note: v1.28.1 delegated-approval — 'proceed' is matched by is_continuation_request BEFORE the new is_imperative_request branch fires (continuation is checked first); both intents authorize destructive ops via is_execution_intent_value, so the gate behavior is the same as execution. Domain=research because 'recommendation' registers as a research keyword.

prompt_preview: do option C?
note: v1.28.1 delegated-approval disqualifier — trailing ? keeps the question form advisory
